mechanism_narrative: Elevated federal funds rates increase the discount rate for future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative forward S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns are not significantly lower than baseline when rates exceed 5%.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid deterioration in the unemployment rate signals weakening labor demand and impending recession, triggering risk-off positioning that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent quarter.
failure_condition: Forward returns remain positive in >55% of instances following the trigger, or the signal fails to produce a negative rank IC over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand strength, supporting corporate revenue growth and generating positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to produce a positive rank IC over rolling 2-year windows, or returns are negative when both series exceed their 12-month moving averages.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production reflects immediate supply-side capacity utilization and short-term economic momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the following week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in >60% of observations when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.5% month-over-month, or the signal loses predictive power in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation combined with restrictive policy rates creates a stagflationary environment that compresses profit margins and equity valuations, resulting in negative forward returns over the quarter.
failure_condition: The interaction term yields a negative or insignificant coefficient in out-of-sample regression, or forward returns are positive when both thresholds are breached.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in total nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding economic activity and wage growth, which supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, producing positive forward S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.01 over any 3-year rolling window, or forward returns are negative when payroll growth exceeds 100k.

mechanism_narrative: A slowdown in real GDP growth below the 1% threshold signals deteriorating aggregate demand and recession risk, prompting equity market sell-offs that generate negative forward returns.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces positive 20-day returns in >65% of instances following the GDP threshold breach, or the signal fails to separate bull/bear regimes statistically.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent increases in industrial production and decreases in the unemployment rate confirm a synchronized supply and labor market expansion, driving robust corporate earnings and positive forward equity returns.
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to outperform a buy-and-hold benchmark over rolling 2-year periods, or the interaction effect reverses sign when UNRATE exceeds 4.5%.
